name: Firecrawl
mcp_notes: Firecrawl provides an official MCP server, which can be configured in MCP-compatible clients.

evidence.claim: Firecrawl provides an API to search, scrape, and interact with the web, returning LLM-ready data like Markdown or JSON.
The API supports authentication via Bearer tokens using an API key.
A free tier is available, allowing users to access the API without immediate payment.
Search citation: Firecrawl Explained: Turning the Web into Usable Data with AI | ApiX-Drive

access_notes: Developers can sign up for a free tier or use the service in a keyless mode with monthly free credits. Higher usage tiers are available through paid plans.
auth_notes: The API uses Bearer token authentication via an API key.
api_notes: The API is broad, covering core functions like scraping, crawling, searching, and autonomous agent-based data gathering. It is accessible via REST API or official SDKs (Python, Node.js).
one_liner: Firecrawl is an API service that crawls, scrapes, and interacts with the web to provide LLM-ready data for AI agents and applications.
